project.name: Milking Process Compliance Monitor
project.problem_statement: Supervisors cannot verify that milking staff correctly perform every required task (cleaning, stripping, machine attachment, milking, detachment, post-dip) for each cow, leading to inconsistent hygiene/process compliance and no objective way to measure employee performance.
project.proposed_solution: Computer-vision pipeline (YOLOv8-based detection + temporal state machine) monitoring a fixed side camera covering the first two cows of a two-row barn, paired with a Next.js supervisor application for scheduling, live monitoring, statistics, and reporting.
project.mvp_scope_note: This is a PILOT/DEMO scope covering only the two cows visible to the camera, not the full row. Full-row coverage is an explicit Phase 2 item requiring additional cameras.

known_limitations.limitation: Only 2 cows per session are monitored (camera field of view). Remaining cows in both rows are not observed by the system in this pilot phase.
known_limitations.impact: Analytics and compliance reporting apply only to the 2 monitored cows per session, not the full herd.

known_limitations.limitation: Single side-angle camera causes occlusion during close-contact tasks (cleaning, stripping, dipping) since the worker's body blocks the udder from this angle.
known_limitations.impact: System can reliably verify TASK OCCURRENCE, SEQUENCE, and DURATION (presence/motion-based detection), but NOT quality/correctness of execution (e.g. cannot confirm iodine was applied to all 4 teats).

known_limitations.limitation: Stripping's core hygiene purpose (visually checking milk for discoloration/infection) is not verifiable by any camera angle since it requires close inspection of milk in a handheld cup.
known_limitations.impact: System verifies that the stripping ACTION occurred (motion signature + duration at each teat), not the health assessment outcome.

known_limitations.limitation: Employee identity is supervisor-asserted (entered/selected in the multi-step form), not independently verified by hardware (no RFID/badge) or biometrics in MVP.
known_limitations.impact: Per-employee performance stats are only as accurate as the supervisor's assignment. Supervisor can edit the assignment post-hoc if a mistake is noticed on the live dashboard.

known_limitations.limitation: Session start/end is a coarse supervisor-defined window, not a per-cow boundary.
known_limitations.impact: The window tells the inference service when to actively watch; the system itself must auto-detect where one cow's process ends and the next begins within that window.

layer_1.name: Object/region detector
layer_1.purpose: Per-frame detection of: person, hands, cow udder region, iodine/spray bottle, stripping cup, teat cups/milking machine (attached vs detached state), post-dip applicator.
layer_1.output: Bounding boxes + class labels + confidence, per frame.

layer_2.name: Temporal state machine / task-sequence engine
layer_2.purpose: Consumes Layer 1 detections over time to infer task start/end, duration, sequence correctness, and missed tasks. Also infers cow-process boundaries (see hierarchy below).
approach_options: Rule-based state machine (object presence + duration + proximity thresholds) — recommended for MVP, faster to build/debug, easier to tune with supervisors.
Learned temporal model (e.g. lightweight LSTM/temporal-CNN over Layer 1 detections) — candidate for Phase 2 once enough labeled session data exists.

detection_hierarchy.description: Session (supervisor-defined, coarse) contains multiple CowProcesses (model-detected, granular) each containing multiple TaskEvents (model-detected, per task).

camera_setup.position: Side of the two rows, fixed field of view covering the first two cow positions only
camera_setup.resolution: High-resolution (per client), does not resolve occlusion — occlusion is a geometry problem, not a pixel-count problem
camera_setup.scaling_plan: Phase 2: additional cameras/angles per row to (a) cover more cows, (b) reduce occlusion for quality verification

milking_tasks.name: Pre-milking cleaning
milking_tasks.description: Udder and teats cleaned with iodine-based solution and dried.
milking_tasks.detection_signal: Person's hands + spray/cleaning tool detected near udder region, sustained motion for minimum duration.
milking_tasks.verifiable_by_camera: Occurrence and duration only, not thoroughness of cleaning.
milking_tasks.missed_task_rule: No qualifying hand+tool+udder-proximity motion detected for minimum threshold before machine attachment begins.

milking_tasks.name: Stripping
milking_tasks.description: Manual squirting of first streams from each teat into a separate container; checks for discoloration and stimulates oxytocin release.
milking_tasks.detection_signal: Hand + stripping cup detected near udder, characteristic short repeated motion, sustained for minimum duration.
milking_tasks.verifiable_by_camera: Occurrence and duration only. Discoloration/health check outcome is NOT verifiable (see LIM-03).
milking_tasks.missed_task_rule: No qualifying stripping motion signature detected before machine attachment.

milking_tasks.name: Machine attachment
milking_tasks.description: Teat cups attached, vacuum system begins pulsation.
milking_tasks.detection_signal: Milking machine/teat-cup object transitions from 'detached' to 'attached' state near udder.
milking_tasks.verifiable_by_camera: High confidence — clear object state change.
milking_tasks.missed_task_rule: Machine never transitions to attached state within session window for this cow.

milking_tasks.name: Milking (active extraction)
milking_tasks.description: Machine pulsates ~40x/min, typically 5-7 minutes per cow.
milking_tasks.detection_signal: Machine remains in attached state; duration timer running.
milking_tasks.verifiable_by_camera: Duration is directly measurable. Pulsation rate verification would require machine telemetry, not camera (flagged as out of MVP scope).
milking_tasks.missed_task_rule: Attached duration significantly below expected range (configurable threshold, default flags <3 min as anomaly for supervisor review, not auto-fail).

milking_tasks.name: Machine detachment
milking_tasks.description: Machine detached, often automatically once flow stops.
milking_tasks.detection_signal: Milking machine/teat-cup object transitions from 'attached' to 'detached' state.
milking_tasks.verifiable_by_camera: High confidence — clear object state change.
milking_tasks.missed_task_rule: N/A (implied by process end) — flagged only if attachment never ends within a reasonable max duration (possible equipment issue alert).

milking_tasks.name: Post-dip disinfectant application
milking_tasks.description: Post-dip disinfectant applied to teats after detachment.
milking_tasks.detection_signal: Hand + dip applicator detected near udder shortly after machine detachment.
milking_tasks.verifiable_by_camera: Occurrence and duration only, not coverage of all teats.
milking_tasks.missed_task_rule: No qualifying dip-application motion detected within a configurable window after detachment, before the worker moves away from the cow.

cow_process_boundary_detection.start_signal: First qualifying task motion (TASK-01 signature) detected at a monitored cow position with no currently-active CowProcess for that position.
cow_process_boundary_detection.end_signal: TASK-06 completed (or timeout) AND worker/motion detected moving away from that cow position.
cow_process_boundary_detection.note: This boundary detection is effectively the same detection problem as task detection, one level coarser — it wraps the task state machine in an outer 'cow present / process active / process complete' state, not a separate system.

training_data_requirements.minimum_scope: Multiple employees, multiple lighting/weather conditions, both monitored cow positions, wet/dirty barn conditions represented.
training_data_requirements.labeling_need: Segment-level (start/end timestamp) labels per task per cow-process, not just bounding boxes — this labeling is significantly more labor-intensive than standard YOLO box labeling and should be budgeted accordingly.
training_data_requirements.recommendation: Start with a small labeled pilot set to validate the rule-based state machine thresholds before investing in large-scale labeling.

inference_service.why_separate_from_web_app: Next.js cannot run real-time YOLOv8 inference on a live RTSP stream in production. A dedicated service is required.
inference_service.proposed_stack: Python, FastAPI, Ultralytics YOLOv8, OpenCV for RTSP ingestion and frame-splitting (2 ROIs, one per monitored cow position)
data_flow: Inference service splits frame into 2 ROIs (cow position 1, cow position 2)
Layer 1 detection per ROI per frame
Layer 2 state machine tracks CowProcess + TaskEvent state per ROI
On state change (task started/completed/missed, cow process started/ended), service emits event to backend (webhook or message queue)
Backend persists event to Postgres and pushes update to dashboard via WebSocket/SSE
inference_service.trigger_model: Inference service is active during the supervisor-defined session window (coarse start/end), not solely trusting the schedule for task-level timing — actual task/process boundaries are auto-detected within that window.

entities.name: Employee
entities.note: Simple roster entity for MVP; selected/assigned by supervisor in the multi-step form. No independent identity verification in MVP (see LIM-04).

entities.name: Session
entities.note: One coarse session = one watch-window. Covers activity for the 2 monitored cow positions only.

entities.name: CowProcess
entities.note: Auto-detected by the ML pipeline, not manually entered.

auth.roles: Supervisor (MVP has a single role; multi-role/RBAC is a Phase 2 consideration if the client wants employee logins too)

multi_step_form.purpose: Supervisor schedules a monitoring session before milking begins.
multi_step_form.validation: Zod schema validation on client and server; sanitize all text inputs before persistence.

dashboard_realtime.purpose: Supervisor views live status of the active session.
displays: Per monitored cow position: current task in progress, task-by-task completion checklist, missed-task alerts as they occur
Ability to edit assigned employee if a discrepancy is noticed
dashboard_realtime.transport: WebSocket or Server-Sent Events, fed by backend events originating from the inference service.

metrics: Missed tasks count and breakdown by task type
Filter by date range, employee, cow position
statistics_page.export: Downloadable Word (.docx) report summarizing the above for a selected session or date range.

email.content: Session summary, per-cow-process task completion status, list of any missed tasks, task durations.

decorative_optional: Three.js — decorative dashboard/landing flair only, explicitly NOT core to MVP functionality, build after core features are stable
realtime: WebSocket or SSE for dashboard live updates
Redis/BullMQ or equivalent queue between inference service and backend (recommended for reliability, avoids dropped events)
security: Middleware-based rate limiting on API routes (especially auth and form submission endpoints)
Input validation/sanitization via Zod on all form and API inputs (XSS/injection protection)
Server-side authorization checks (supervisor can only view/edit their own sessions unless multi-tenant scoping says otherwise)
reporting: docx (Node library) for Word report generation
Resend or Nodemailer for transactional email

open_questions_for_client: Is the 2-monitored-cow pilot scope acceptable for launch, with full-row coverage explicitly deferred to a funded Phase 2?
Confirm acceptance that the system verifies task occurrence/sequence/duration, not execution quality (e.g. thoroughness of cleaning or dip coverage).
Any regulatory/consent considerations if biometric identification (face recognition) is pursued in a future phase?

Expected minimum/maximum duration per task, to calibrate anomaly/missed-task thresholds — needs a subject-matter conversation with an experienced milking supervisor, not just the general process description.

phase_1_mvp: Rule-based state machine for the 6 tasks across 2 monitored cow positions
phase_2_candidates: Additional cameras for full-row coverage and reduced occlusion
Learned temporal model replacing/augmenting rule-based state machine
